notes: CRITICAL: Existing schema has battery_alerts (line 2079) with overlapping columns (severity, alert_type, message, acknowledged, acknowledged_at, acknowledged_by, value, threshold, device_id, created_at). Reviewer must decide whether telemetry_alerts is (a) additive (new rule-based persistence with cds_flagged, payload jsonb, notified_to jsonb, resolved_at), or (b) should EXTEND battery_alerts with the missing fields (rule, triggered_at, resolved_at, payload, notified_to, cds_flagged) and reuse the existing table.

BRD 6.2.6 lists 8 named rules with severity tiers and notification fan-out — telemetry_alerts is justified as a rule-based store. battery_alerts uses alert_type+message rather than rule+payload; semantics differ.
BRD sync audit (line 495 of /tmp/nbfc_extract/sync_audit.txt) does not call out telemetry_alerts vs battery_alerts; this is a NEW collision surfaced by the auditor.
AC4 + AC5 require an offline-scan job that fires on absence of packets — depends on a cron worker (Vercel cron); ensure the cron is registered separately from the per-packet evaluator.
